programme.desc: Documentary following the Duke of Cambridge's work to break the taboo surrounding mental health, using football as a way to encourage men to open up and talk about their emotions. The documentary follows the Duke as he meets players and fans from grassroots to the elite of the game, and openly discusses their mental health challenges. Former England goalkeeper Joe Hart talks about how he has learnt to cope with difficulties at the very top of the game, and William hears from a group of bereaved fathers who use their local football team as a support network and safe space to talk. Former Premier League footballer Marvin Sordell opens up about his struggles with depression, while Chelsea manager Frank Lampard compares life now with his early experiences of professional football.(n)

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: Saying goodbye to Puducherry, the celebs journey north to the foothills of the Himalayas for their final week in India. Following a treacherous drive up narrow, winding roads, they arrive at their new home on the outskirts of Rishikesh, ready to experience the spiritual side of life in Northern India. After starting the next day with an outdoor group meditation session among the wild monkeys and spectacular views, Duncan and John waste no time exploring the bustling town of Rishikesh, which attracts thousands of pilgrims from all over the world.(n)

programme.desc: A princess with the power to freeze anything she touches loses control of her abilities and plunges the kingdom into a perpetual winter. Disney animated fantasy adventure, with the voices of Kristen Bell and Idina Menzel.(n)

Later, he returns and takes hostages in the emergency department, and Charlie puts his life on the line to try and protect those around him. Meanwhile, Dylan risks his life for Faith, which makes her question her emotions towards him, and a frustrated Will asks Fenisha to give him a second chance.(n)

programme.title: Our Coast
programme.desc: Adrian Chiles, Mehreen Baig and a team of experts explore four spectacular coastlines linked by the Irish Sea, and meet the people who call them home. Each programme features people local to each region, as they fully immerse in the coastal way of life, from tackling unique jobs to experiencing magical close encounters with wildlife. The first focuses on the Merseyside coast, which stretches from Sefton Sands in the north to the Wirral in the south, via the port city of Liverpool.(n)

programme.title: Earth's Seasonal Secrets
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how animals all over the world rise to the challenge of surviving the winter. While some creatures face an up-hill struggle to survive, a colder climate plays to the advantage of others, who quickly set about making use of inventive means to seize the huge opportunities the season brings. Caribou become 'ice road' travellers as the ground under-foot becomes slippery, and stoats build their own fur beds, snow monkeys set about finding warm baths, and even Emperor penguins - who are build to face extreme low temperatures - find their own way to endure the world's most savage winter conditions. Narrated by Andrew Scott.(n)

programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc hosts the game show in which pairs of contestants go head to head attempting to crack a series of passwords, in a bid to win £2,500.(n)

programme.title: Big Dreams Small Spaces
programme.desc: In Brighton, amateur horticulturists Helen and Dax have their hands full with twins, one of who has special needs. Their current garden is a building site, but armed with advice from presenter Monty Don they attack the challenge head on, and a trip to the Chelsea Flower Show also proves useful. Meanwhile in Buckinghamshire, Valerie's 'concrete driveway' of a garden has her stumped - she has no idea how to plant in or on it. A visit to an inspirational pottery puts her back on track, but with so many choices can she decide on the right ones?(n)

programme.title: Mr Blandings Builds His Dream House (1948)
programme.desc: A city couple set out to build a home in the wilds of rural Connecticut. They discover they have bitten off more than they can chew thanks to a host of legal problems and an apparently endless stream of cowboy workmen and high-priced materials. Comedy, starring Cary Grant, Myrna Loy, Melvyn Douglas and Reginald Denny.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At St Albans Cathedral and Abbey, Hertfordshire, Claire Rawle discovers an arts and crafts bowl that may have belonged to George Bernard Shaw's wife, and Jonathan Pratt finds a drawing by the artist Laura Knight. Presenter Paul Martin heads underground as he explores Royston Cave, which boasts medieval carvings.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble present as a breeding pair of rare Amur tigers arrive at the park, an oryx gives keepers the run around and the giraffes move into their summer pastures.(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: Mary and her guests examine the links between risk, culture and creativity. Director Lee Daniels explains why he thinks the current shutdown in Hollywood could be a radical creative opportunity for film-makers, and photographer Don McCullin questions the worth of a career spent risking his own life to alert the world to the suffering of warfare. Comedian and 'guilty feminist' Deborah Frances-White joins Mary and David Spiegelhalter, Professor of the Public Understanding of Risk, to disentangle the complexities of emerging into life after lockdown, and artists Gillian Wearing and Michael Landy sign off the show with their first collaboration in more than 20 years.(n)

programme.title: War Walks
programme.desc: Historian Richard Holmes describes the events of May 1940, when dozens of small-scale actions combined to delay the Germans taking Dunkirk, and visits the beaches and breakwaters from which thousands of British troops narrowly escaped capture.(n)

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Live from their home bases in the New Forest, Cornwall, West Wales and the River Thames, Chris Packham, Gillian Burke, Iolo Williams and Steve Backshall share the latest stories from the wildlife cameras. Chris reveals how fish have more ways of making sound than any other vertebrate on the planet, Iolo discusses the intimate secrets of the bird families he has been following in his own back garden, and Michaela Strachan relives the dramatic story of a very skilful stoat in Minsmere in 2016. There is also the extraordinary spectacle of the firework sea anemone, and a rare reef made by beautiful peacock worms.(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts the return of the quiz with a difference, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers she finds most interesting. The show has now reached the letter R, and begins with a rather Rude episode. Joining Sandi are John Barrowman, Phill Jupitus, Aisling Bea and regular panellist Alan Davies.(n)

programme.title: The First Team
programme.desc: Comedy written by Iain Morris and Damon Beesley - creators of The Inbetweeners - following the adventures of three young footballers, their Italian manager and eccentric chairman. New signing Mattie Sullivan arrives at the club, but a meeting with chairman Mark Crane leads him to wonder if he's been signed by mistake. As if that wasn't bad enough, club hard-man Petey Brooks takes a dislike to him and legendary Italian manager Cesare is nowhere to be found. Starring Shaquille Ali-Yebuah, Will Arnett, Jack McMullen, Jake Short and Theo Barklem-Biggs.(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Another leading performer on the stand-up circuit plays host in this show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo, warming up the audience and then introducing routines by fellow comedians ready to share their light-hearted views on modern life.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett.(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Adventures
programme.desc: Eric Monkman and Bobby Seagull continues their travels around Britain, this time seeking out the birthplaces of the inventions that fuelled the nation's industrial infrastructure. Their first stop is the Royal Institution in London, where they examine how the Davy lamp saved the lives of thousands of coal miners, then pay a visit to the preserved laboratory of Michael Faraday, another of their scientific heroes, whose research on electricity paved the way for its application as a ubiquitous energy supply.(n)

programme.title: Peter Sellers: A State of Comic Ecstasy
programme.desc: The comedy pioneer behind the Goon Show, Dr Strangelove and the Pink Panther series is explored in depth in this film surveying his meteoric rise to fame and troubled personal life. The film charts his formative years backstage as part of his parents' itinerant music hall revue group, his years of wartime service in India and Burma, through to his global superstardom. His two surviving children Sarah and Victoria recall the challenges of growing up alongside his mood swings, while his grandson Will explores the troubled legacy his grandfather left behind.(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Mathematician Hannah Fry and doctor and broadcaster Michael Mosley join an Imperial College, London, experiment to assess the brainpower of the nation, putting members of the public to the test in a battle of wits as they pit young and old, males and females, and tech lovers and readers against one other. The results reveal new science about how people's intelligence changes through their lives, while Hannah and Michael examine groundbreaking new science on how lifestyle and love of technology is changing the human brain.(n)

programme.title: Jay Blades' Home Fix

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: A Letchworth couple receive a surprise £4,500 garden makeover as a thank you from their son - a challenge that Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers find difficult to resist.(n)

programme.title: Hubble: The Wonders of Space Revealed
programme.desc: Since its launch in April 1990, the Hubble space telescope has made more than 1.5 million observations of the universe. One of the most productive scientific instruments in history, over the past three decades it has transformed man's view of the cosmos. This documentary tells the craft's remarkable story, drawing upon contributions by the astronauts who spacewalked on Hubble to carry out critical repairs and upgrades and the astronomers who have used its unique powers to make new discoveries about the universe.(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.sub-title: A Bear with a Bounty: Natural World
programme.desc: Lily is a wild black bear whose life has been documented online since the day of her birth by biologist Dr Lynn Rogers, who has a unique relationship with all the bears in his study, feeding them by hand and walking with them in the woods of north Minnesota. However, his methods have divided opinion and Lily's fame has resulted in her becoming a prime target when the six-week bear hunting season begins. This film follows possibly the most challenging chapter in Lily's remarkable life. Tamsin Greig narrates.(n)

programme.title: Big Dreams Small Spaces

Ant and Jake in Bath want to create a Japanese-style design for their L-shaped plot, but events take a dramatic turn when they discover an underlying problem with their soil that calls for an extreme solution.(n)

programme.title: King Kong (1933)
programme.desc: A film producer and his crew visit a remote island where they encounter a 50ft gorilla which takes a shine to their leading lady. When they decide to take it back to America, they fail to foresee the inevitable havoc as the gigantic ape runs amok in New York. Fantasy, starring Fay Wray, Robert Armstrong and Bruce Cabot.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts Adam Partridge and Charles Hanson value items brought to the Platform in Morecambe, Lancashire, including a Russian watercolour found in a car boot sale. Plus, presenter Paul Martin discovers the story of Leyland, Britain's biggest motor company.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Road to Mexico
programme.desc: For Mexicans, their most revered dishes come from the south, and their Mecca is Oaxaca, where locals still dress in traditional costume. Rick explores the home of Mexico's national cheese, Queso Oaxacana. It's also a land where vanilla grows wild and cacao orchards are harvested to make superb chocolate.(n)

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: The team reviews the extraordinary events that have taken place during the week, as Gillian Burke looks back at her time with the Beaver family in Cornwall, Chris Packham reveals the extraordinary transformation young salmon perform and Iolo Williams reveals the latest findings about the mysterious migrations of Short Eared Owls. Plus, Steve Backshall introduces his young two-year-old son to a local family of swans.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don reveals how to pot tomatoes on to the next stage of their growth. He also pots up tender vegetables and plants containers for summer colour. Adam Frost enjoys the seasonal plants in his garden and shares plans to transform a part of his plot into a seating area using a few logs and plants. Arit Anderson fills a range of imaginative containers with a variety of plants, while Frances Tophill explores spring flowering trees and their benefits for bees. Plus, how one man has designed his home around his love of houseplants.(n)

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: The music programme continues in its lockdown incarnation as Jools chats from his south London studio via videolink to Golden Globe-winning actor, comedian, musician and writer Hugh Laurie, inviting him to recount his musical journey. Laurie's picks reflect the influences that can be found on his own two records Let Them Talk and Didn't It Rain, the former featuring collaborations with Tom Jones, Irma Thomas and Dr John. As usual, there's also time for Jools to focus on new music, with a unique performance by multi-instrumentalist and singer Jacob Collier, who will be making his debut on the show.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den: Best Ever Pitches
programme.desc: Joe Lycett narrates a compilation of some of the most memorable moments from the past 17 series of the business ideas contest, featuring Dragons past and present getting to grips with ingenious inventions and bizarre money-making schemes. The show recalls some of the harshest dismissals, but also the times when entrepreneurs got the chance to make their dream a reality.(n)

programme.title: Trumbo (2015)
programme.desc: Biopic of the acclaimed Hollywood screenwriter, who was jailed and blacklisted in the 1940s for his left-wing political views. On his release from prison, he manages to continue writing using a series of pseudonyms, and his newfound success leads many to question the validity of the blacklist. Biopic of the writer, starring Bryan Cranston, Diane Lane and Helen Mirren.(n)

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.sub-title: The Best of RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Sophie Raworth and Joe Swift kick off a week of special programmes celebrating past stagings of the horticultural event by reflecting on a decade of Best Show Garden award winners. Mary Berry champions one of the greatest rose growers in the Great Pavilion, while Arit Anderson looks at the trendiest houseplants and there is also the launch of the BBC RHS People's Choice of the Decade vote, where the public get to vote on their favourite People's Choice winning garden of the past 10 years.(n)

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues to explore Alaska, from the remote former goldrush settlement of Talkeetna to the railwayless state capital, Juneau. Along the way, he helps a homesteader fell a tree to clear land for a log cabin, discovers how 100 years ago photographers captured the beauty of the Alaskan landscape and meets scientists studying the Northern Lights.(n)

programme.desc: A look back at some of Lindsey Russell and Richie Driss's most adrenaline-fuelled challenges.(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall explores the world of parasites, including tapeworms, mosquitoes and cuckoos, investigating the skills they use to exploit their hosts.(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall investigates the skills and senses that creatures around the world use to hunt and survive. This programme focuses on the speed and acceleration of the cheetah, the elastic tongue and 360-degree vision of the chameleon, and a shark with a hidden sixth sense.(n)

programme.title: Miniature Britain
programme.desc: Biologist George McGavin travels around Britain with a revolutionary new microscope camera to provide a close-up view of the natural world. The film reveals how caterpillars' feet have hooks that anchor them upside down to leaves, butterfly and moth wings are a kaleidoscope of colourful scales that deter predators, and birds' feathers have thousands of hooks that zip together to keep them airborne. The film-makers also show miniature life in cities, such as creatures grazing the moss on pavements and the legions of dust mites scavenging for food in everyday homes.(n)

programme.title: Ice Age Giants
programme.desc: Alice Roberts sets out to discover why the creatures that dominated the planet 40,000 years ago died out as the world started to warm up and the end of the Ice Age neared. She examines how the woolly rhino was unable to adapt when the increase in snowfall prevented it eating enough grass, and learns that mastodons turned on one another.(n)

programme.title: The Good Dinosaur (2015)
programme.desc: In a world where the asteroid impact that wiped out the dinosaurs never happened, humans have evolved as a primitive species while the giant lizards remain the dominant life form on Earth. A young apatosaurus becomes separated from his family, but finds a friend in a feral human boy as he makes the dangerous journey home. Pixar animated adventure, with the voices of Raymond Ochoa and Jack Bright.(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for the Factory
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the living history series in which a group of women step back in time to experience factory life in the past. The year has moved on to 1973, when manufacturing output and productivity was at its highest since the war. The women are tasked with bright orange flares, a must-have fashion item of the decade, and with business booming, targets have to be met. However, when a male machinist joins the production line, his end-of-week pay packet sends shockwaves around the factory.(n)

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Chef Brian Turner takes guests back to their youths by visiting locations important to them and then cooking dishes inspired by their childhoods. In the first edition he is joined by cookery writer and former Great British Bake Off judge Mary Berry.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A retrospective look at the television appearances made over the years by Days of Wine and Roses actor Jack Lemmon, capturing the highlights and milestones of his life and career. Narrated by actress Sylvia Syms.(n)

programme.title: Some Like It Hot (1959)
programme.desc: After witnessing a Mob hit in Chicago, two male jazz musicians don drag and hide out with an all-girl band to evade the gangsters pursuing them. However, the pair struggle to disguise their attraction for the beautiful lead singer - while one of them catches the eye of a smitten millionaire. Billy Wilder's classic romantic comedy, starring Jack Lemmon, Tony Curtis and Marilyn Monroe.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at television appearances made over the years by American film actor Tony Curtis, capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career, which led to him becoming considered a Hollywood star. Narrated by Sylvia Syms.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking them to auction.(n)

programme.title: Dam Busters: The Race to Smash the German Dams
programme.desc: James Holland presents an analysis of the 1943 air attacks by the RAF, in which 19 Lancaster bombers ventured into the industrial heartland of Germany to destroy three heavily defended dams with the new bouncing bomb. He argues that the raid was the best-executed and most important of all time and explores whether its true impact has been underestimated - and if the result should have been even greater.(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: The members of Walmington-on-Sea's Home Guard platoon are excited when they hear they have been selected to appear in an army training film - but Captain Mainwaring refuses to take part when he finds out he and his men are to play Nazi soldiers. Classic comedy first aired in 1975, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier.(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the growth of Kuala Lumpur, from a swampland tin mine to a major British colony, before becoming a major economic powerhouse in its own right. He meets jewellery at the Royal Selangor pewter factory, samples Malaysia's national dish, and investigates the scandalous behaviour of an infamous British resident general at the turn of the 20th century. He then heads to Melaka, for centuries one of the greatest trading ports in the world, before finally arriving at the tip of the Malaysian peninsula in the historic city of Johor Bahru.(n)

programme.title: Ella Fitzgerald: Just One of Those Things
programme.desc: The life and times of the jazz singer, beginning with a 1934 talent contest at the Apollo Theatre in Harlem, and following her next five decades, reflecting the passions and troubles of the times in which she lived in her life and her music. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo, with contributions from Laura Mvula, Ray Brown Jr, Tony Bennett, Johnny Mathis, Jamie Cullum, Cleo Laine and Smokey Robinson.(n)

programme.title: Jazz Divas Gold
programme.desc: A selection of archive performances by acclaimed female jazz artists from shows including Not So Much a Programme - More a Way of Life, Morecambe and Wise, Parkinson and Show of the Week. Featuring Ella Fitzgerald, Peggy Lee, Blossom Dearie, Nina Simone, Cleo Laine, Marion Montgomery, Betty Carter, Sarah Vaughan, Eartha Kitt and Amy Winehouse.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints a tropical seascape - an exotic ocean masterpiece complete with palm trees swaying in the warm breeze.(n)

programme.title: Danceworks
programme.sub-title: Danceworks: Maria Pagés - An Ode to Flamenco
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the work of flamenco star María Pagés, as she prepares a haunting new choreography for a performance in her birthplace of Seville.(n)

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Rejected by Elizabeth, Darcy decides to return to Rosling Park - and resolves to reveal the truth about Wickham's character in a letter. Colin Firth, Jennifer Ehle, Alison Steadman, Benjamin Whitrow and Julia Sawalha star.(n)

programme.title: Earth from Space
programme.desc: Cameras in space tell stories of life on our planet from a brand new perspective, to reveal new discoveries and explore Earth's most remote habitats. In the third episode, cameras unveil stories behind some of the planet's most intriguing colours, including the lights of the aurora, which tower in vertical streaks hundreds of kilometres high. Elsewhere, cameras also capture vast phytoplankton blooms in the ocean, and millions of rapeseed flowers in bloom in China's Yunnan province.(n)

programme.title: Unprecedented
programme.desc: Two short plays written and performed in isolation: Grounded, by Duncan Macmillan, tells the story of a woman who is worried about her elderly parents' cavalier attitude to the virus. However, when she is overcome by the stress of her work, it seems that she is the one who needs looking after. Katherine Parkinson, Alison Steadman, Michael Elwyn and Arlo Dollimore star. Fear Fatigue, by Prasanna Puwanarajah, is based on conversations with NHS workers in March 2020, and documents the feelings and fears of frontline staff in the days and weeks before the coronavirus lockdown. Starring Paterson Joseph, Rory Kinnear, Esh Alladi, Natalie Dew, Anna Madeley and Jodie McNee.(n)

programme.title: Unprecedented
programme.desc: Three new lockdown dramas. Kat and Zaccy, written by Deboran Bruce, is about a son ignoring his mother's emotional pleas to come home from university during lockdown. Monica Dolan and Alex Lawther star. The Unexpected Expert, by Matilda Ibini, tells the story of a woman who worries that the crisis will have a huge impact on her social care package. Saida Ahmed, Lucianne McEvoy and Golda Rosheuvel star. The Night After, by Josh Azouz, stars Kathryn Hunter and Marcello Magni as a couple in their sixties who try to make contact with the outside world.(n)

programme.title: Chasing the Moon
programme.desc: As rumours circulated that the Soviets were preparing to send an unmanned spacecraft to the moon, Nasa quickly developed the Gemini programme, sending astronauts into Earth orbit to practice critical manoeuvres for the eventual trip to the Moon.(n)

programme.title: Invasion! With Sam Willis
programme.desc: The historian discovers French plans to invade Britain by balloon, the subterranean fortress built for troops in the 19th century when invasion paranoia was at fever pitch, and examines the rich legacy of the Huguenots.(n)

programme.title: Sleuths, Spies & Sorcerers: Andrew Marr's Paperback Heroes
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the espionage genre, arguing that the British examples of it allows readers to engage with big questions about the human condition. The conventions of writing about spies are gleaned from Frederick Forsyth, William Boyd, Gerald Seymour, Charles Cumming and former MI5 director general Stella Rimington, and Andrew travels to Berlin in the footsteps of John le Carre, whose experience of witnessing the Wall being erected in 1961 inspired him to write The Spy Who Came in from the Cold.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints a tropical seascape - an exotic ocean masterpiece complete with palm trees swaying in the warm breeze.(n)

programme.title: Danceworks
programme.sub-title: Danceworks: Maria Pagés - An Ode to Flamenco
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the work of flamenco star María Pagés, as she prepares a haunting new choreography for a performance in her birthplace of Seville.(n)

programme.title: Earth from Space
programme.desc: Cameras in space tell stories of life on our planet from a brand new perspective, to reveal new discoveries and explore Earth's most remote habitats. In the third episode, cameras unveil stories behind some of the planet's most intriguing colours, including the lights of the aurora, which tower in vertical streaks hundreds of kilometres high. Elsewhere, cameras also capture vast phytoplankton blooms in the ocean, and millions of rapeseed flowers in bloom in China's Yunnan province.(n)

programme.title: Classic Comps
programme.desc: Compilations of classical music performances from the BBC archives. In the first programme, Clemency Burton-Hill introduces string quartets playing music ranging from Mozart and Beethoven to Elvis Costello and Pete Townshend in a variety of locations ranging from stately homes to helicopters.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Simon Mayo and Andy Crane host the edition first shown on September 14, with performances by artists including Damian, Madonna, Technotronic featuring Felly, Tears for Fears, Sydney Youngblood, Tina Turner, Black Box and Aerosmith.(n)

programme.title: Kermode and Mayo's Home Entertainment Service
programme.desc: Mark Kermode and Simon Mayo discuss the new Netflix series Space Force, starring Steve Carrell and Lisa Kudrow, as well as the hotly anticipated sci-fi thriller The Vast of Night, a new Amazon Studios release. The duo also round up the best - and worst - of the rest of streaming culture across movies and premium television.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the edition first shown on September 21, featuring performances by London Boys, Aerosmith, S'Express, Richard Marx, the Wonder Stuff, WASP, Black Box and Janet Jackson.(n)

programme.title: Rock 'n' Roll America
programme.desc: Documentary examining the most important era in the history of popular music - the birth of rock 'n' roll. The opening programme focuses on the origins of the sound in 1950s America when the rhythm-driven mix of blues, boogie woogie and vocal harmony was nurtured by small independent record labels and championed by young music pioneers such as Fats Domino and Little Richard. This episode also looks at the start of Elvis Presley's career in Memphis and examines the impact the film industry had on the rock movement. Contributors include Tom Jones, Jerry Lee Lewis and Don Everly.(n)

programme.title: Classic Albums
programme.sub-title: Classic Albums: The Crickets - The 'Chirping' Crickets
programme.desc: The making of the debut album by the Crickets, and the only one featuring Buddy Holly released during his tragically brief career, which ended with his accidental death aged 22. It survives as the purest testament to his skill and diversity as a singer, a pioneering guitar player and not least as a songwriter in an age when few of his peers composed their own material. This documentary celebrates its success and assesses its influence. Contributors include former band member Jerry Allison, singer Cliff Richard and writers John Beecher and Frank Blanas.(n)

programme.title: It's Only Rock 'n' Roll: Rock 'n' Roll at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of classic rock 'n' roll artists and songs in celebration of the musical genre, Including performances by Jerry Lee Lewis, Dion, Dick Dale and Neil Sedaka. Plus, a selection of songs with rock 'n' roll in the title, such as Tom Petty's Anything That's Rock 'n' Roll, Joan Jett's I love Rock 'n' Roll and Oasis's Rock 'n' Roll Star.(n)

programme.title: Singer-Songwriters at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive performances from the 1960s and 1970s, featuring Leonard Cohen, Julie Felix, Sandy Denny, John Martyn, Don McLean, Tim Buckley, Stealers Wheel, Joni Mitchell, Elton John, Paul Simon, Rab Noakes, Loudon Wainwright III, Clifford T Ward and Cat Stevens.(n)

programme.title: Country Music by Ken Burns
programme.desc: How country music adapted to the cultural changes of immediate post-war society between 1945 and 1953. Bill Monroe, Lester Flatt and Earl Scruggs transformed the traditional string band music into something more syncopated - bluegrass. And out of the bars and juke joints came honky-tonk, a new sound with electric guitars and songs about drinking, cheating and heartbreak. Contributors include Kris Kristofferson, Brenda Lee, Ricky Skaggs and Vince Gill. Narrated by Peter Coyote.(n)

programme.title: Rock 'n' Roll America
programme.desc: Documentary examining the most important era in the history of popular music - the birth of rock 'n' roll. The opening programme focuses on the origins of the sound in 1950s America when the rhythm-driven mix of blues, boogie woogie and vocal harmony was nurtured by small independent record labels and championed by young music pioneers such as Fats Domino and Little Richard. This episode also looks at the start of Elvis Presley's career in Memphis and examines the impact the film industry had on the rock movement. Contributors include Tom Jones, Jerry Lee Lewis and Don Everly.(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Documentary filmed over the course of a year following life on five farms around Scotland. It is autumn on Lewis in the Outer Hebrides and sea shepherd Sandy gathers in a flock of wild sheep by boat, while on the mainland in the east Mel and Martin head to market to look for the perfect rams to impregnate their ewes. In central Scotland near Loch Lomond, hill farmers Bobby and Anne health-check their flock to make sure they are fit to breed, and in the far north John has 4,000 ewes to check, but is also expecting thousands of visitors from all over the world as he hosts the World Sheepdog Trials.(n)

programme.title: Masters of the Pacific Coast: The Tribes of the American Northwest
programme.desc: Part two of two. Archaeologist Dr Jago Cooper reveals how the American Northwest cultural tradition managed to survive against the odds. After European contact, indigenous people of what is now southwest Alaska, British Colombia and Washington State suffered disease, theft and oppression, and Cooper argues that Northwest Coast culture has an extraordinary resilience.(n)

programme.title: The Young Montalbano
programme.desc: In a prequel to Inspector Montalbano, the detective moves to a remote village in the Sicilian mountains, where he investigates the case of an attempted murder at the hands of a vulnerable woman, whose motives appear unfathomable. Italian drama series set in the early 1990s, starring Michele Riondino.(n)

programme.title: Own the Sky: Jet Pack Dreamers
programme.desc: The story of Australian David Mayman, whose passion for the tantalising possibilities of jetpacks became an obsession. Filmed over 10 years, the documentary follows his seemingly impossible quest to realise his childhood dream to build and fly the world's first jetpack - an ambition that almost cost him his life.(n)

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: When Francine needs money for new soccer cleats, Muffy hires her as a personal assistant. However, it remains to be seen whether drinking pumpkin smoothies and playing games with a best friend the best way to make an honest buck.(n)

programme.title: Football, Prince William and Our Mental Health
programme.desc: Documentary following the Duke of Cambridge's work to break the taboo surrounding mental health, using football as a way to encourage men to open up and talk about their emotions. The documentary follows the Duke as he meets players and fans from grassroots to the elite of the game, and openly discusses their mental health challenges. Former England goalkeeper Joe Hart talks about how he has learnt to cope with difficulties at the very top of the game, and William hears from a group of bereaved fathers who use their local football team as a support network and safe space to talk. Former Premier League footballer Marvin Sordell opens up about his struggles with depression, while Chelsea manager Frank Lampard compares life now with his early experiences of professional football.(n)

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: Saying goodbye to Puducherry, the celebs journey north to the foothills of the Himalayas for their final week in India. Following a treacherous drive up narrow, winding roads, they arrive at their new home on the outskirts of Rishikesh, ready to experience the spiritual side of life in Northern India. After starting the next day with an outdoor group meditation session among the wild monkeys and spectacular views, Duncan and John waste no time exploring the bustling town of Rishikesh, which attracts thousands of pilgrims from all over the world.(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and green-fingered siblings Harry and David Rich travel to Milton Keynes to compete for the chance to create a garden for husband and wife Vinay and Raunak. Raunak, meanwhile, is more interested in filling the garden with vivid and dramatic colours, to match the style of decor she has used throughout their house. programme.desc: Live coverage as people around the UK applaud health service workers battling the coronavirus pandemic.(n)

programme.title: Football, Prince William and Our Mental Health
programme.desc: Documentary following the Duke of Cambridge's work to break the taboo surrounding mental health, using football as a way to encourage men to open up and talk about their emotions. The documentary follows the Duke as he meets players and fans from grassroots to the elite of the game, and openly discusses their mental health challenges. Former England goalkeeper Joe Hart talks about how he has learnt to cope with difficulties at the very top of the game, and William hears from a group of bereaved fathers who use their local football team as a support network and safe space to talk. Former Premier League footballer Marvin Sordell opens up about his struggles with depression, while Chelsea manager Frank Lampard compares life now with his early experiences of professional football.(n)

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: Saying goodbye to Puducherry, the celebs journey north to the foothills of the Himalayas for their final week in India. Following a treacherous drive up narrow, winding roads, they arrive at their new home on the outskirts of Rishikesh, ready to experience the spiritual side of life in Northern India. After starting the next day with an outdoor group meditation session among the wild monkeys and spectacular views, Duncan and John waste no time exploring the bustling town of Rishikesh, which attracts thousands of pilgrims from all over the world.(n)

programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and green-fingered siblings Harry and David Rich travel to Milton Keynes to compete for the chance to create a garden for husband and wife Vinay and Raunak. Both want the garden to represent the Hindu religion's belief in the power and importance of nature.(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: When ex-con Mick realises his son, Connor, is being moved to America, he desperately pulls a gun on his social worker, Ruth. In an attempt to escape, Mick shoots Ruth in the leg, and flees the hospital. Later, he returns and takes hostages in the emergency department, and Charlie puts his life on the line to try and protect those around him. Meanwhile, Dylan risks his life for Faith, which makes her question her emotions towards him, and a frustrated Will asks Fenisha to give him a second chance.(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes gets the wrong end of the stick and fears her relatives are planning to send her to a nursing home. Furious, she sets out to persuade them she is not going senile - unaware it is actually Spartacus the dog who is set to be sent away. Comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll.(n)

Meanwhile, Dylan risks his life for Faith, which makes her question her emotions towards him, and a frustrated Will asks Fenisha to give him a second chance.(n)

programme.desc: Calum French's over-indulgence has not gone un-noticed in Fraochy Bay, though he is blissfully unaware until he hears a knock at the door. An intervention is about to take place and it's certain that poor Calum has never experienced anything like it.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates another comical selection of mishaps sent in by viewers, featuring accidents filmed using the latest technology only - no camcorder clips or pixelated phone videos in sight. Tonight, a man is accidentally tasered, a couple set their kitchen on fire, and a toddler in an electric car runs amok in a shopping centre.(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.sub-title: Scammers: Hunting the Lockdown Criminals - Tonight
programme.desc: There has been a spike in internet fraud - from fake goods and services to con artists impersonating the authorities through emails, texts, calls and even appearing at people's front doors. Paul Connolly investigates the unscrupulous crooks using the lockdown to prey on others.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family
programme.desc: Anne 'The Governess' Hegerty presents as eight celebrity families take part in the knockout tournament, hoping to win £25,000 for their chosen charity and the coveted title of Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family. The first episode is a battle of the soaps, as Coronation Street's Lucy Fallon (Bethany Platt) and her family take on former EastEnders star Shaun Williamson (Barry Evans) and his clan. Two new rounds have been added to this celebrity spin on the format - including an Observation round featuring cartoon images of Anne that put their memories to the test.(n)

programme.title: Martin Clunes: My Travels and Other Animals
programme.sub-title: The Good, The Bad and the Ugly
programme.desc: Martin learns about animal exploitation - the good, the bad and the ugly. The actor visits Sri Lanka, where fishing for manta rays is legal, but a turtle sanctuary offers hope. In Thailand, he learns more about silk production - as well as a more animal-friendly alternative - and on the Australian Friday Island, he is shown the delicate art of growing pearls. Finally, in the Abrolhos islands, Martin learns about the most valuable catch in Australia's waters.(n)

programme.title: Police Tapes
programme.sub-title: The Murder of Rhys Jones: Police Tapes
programme.desc: When 11-year-old Rhys Jones was shot dead on his way home from weekly football practice in 2007, his family was left shattered and police were left with a case that seemed impenetrable. But when certain names were mentioned again and again, the police planted secret bugs in the homes of these gang members. Here, Susanna Reid gains unprecedented access to the subsequent recordings, where those responsible admit their part and the lengths they would go to cover up the crime. She also talks to Rhys's parents, Mel and Steve, about the impact of this appalling crime.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Shona visits Gail and Sarah, but is clearly confused, while Alya visits Yasmeen in prison and assures her that she knows Geoff abused her, but Yasmeen refuses to discuss it. The doctor tells Leanne that Oliver's test results won't be back for a few weeks and he's free to go home, but while Leanne's delighted, convinced he's improving, Steve and Nick mask their concern.(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: A couple of the birds are forced to fly this week when one of them becomes fed up of being treated like a doormat. It seems Tracey has had enough of visits from Dorien's gentleman callers to her home, and is equally unhappy that her sister doesn't contribute to the household finances, so she throws them both out. The pair of them are soon holed up together in Sharon's old bedsit, where they decide to take a united stand - but things are never quite that straightforward. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph.(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective is brought in to investigate when a former Met police officer is stabbed to death outside a nightclub in Newcastle. The team initially suspects a crime of passion after it emerges the victim had recently rekindled his affair with his childhood sweetheart, whose husband has a history of violence. However, Vera uncovers evidence pointing to a more complex case involving inside information and the hostile takeover of a brewery. What did the deceased find out that cost him his life? Guest starring Jill Halfpenny, Christine Bottomley, Ralph Ineson and Liam Cunningham.(n)

programme.desc: Magoo thinks Weasel has opened a restaurant, and wants to help him improve it. He mistakes the FizzLab for a kitchen!(n)

programme.desc: The Mikkelsens have pizza and the raccoons will do anything to get it. Can Mike hold them off for long enough for his owners to enjoy their dinner?(n)

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: The raccoons break into the Mikkelsens' and Mercury stumbles upon a didgeridoo and just can't get enough of it. Can Mike shut them up?(n)

programme.desc: Bean looks back over his life, recalling how he acquired his beloved teddy bear and learned to drive. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson.(n)

programme.desc: A mysterious furry pink animal moves in with Bean, who doesn't know what it is but can't bring himself to get rid of such an odd creature.(n)

programme.title: The Epic Tales of Captain Underpants
programme.sub-title: Captain Underpants and the Sickening Fumes of Smartsy Fartsy
programme.desc: The boys create a smart gas cloud to steal Mr Krupp's rulebook, but it turns against them.(n)

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Betty wants a nice photo of Dave and Anna, but the camera George uses happens to belong to Gran, and it's been cursed by an ancient mummy.(n)

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: If there were two things Dave could wish for it would be for Fuzzypeg to smell better, and for Gran to be nicer. Is getting what you want always a good thing?(n)

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James presents more highlights from the series, as well as preparing prawn biryani, French onion soup, and fig gateau, and providing a masterclass on steamed sponge pudding. Plus, James' culinary tour of Cornwall and recipes from chefs Clare Smyth, Atul Kochhar and Richard Bertinet.(n)

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by Clare Smyth for a tour of the Lake District. They visit the Windermere Jetty Museum and take a trip on the lake in a traditional steam-powered boat. They also visit the farmers who supply the meat for Clare's restaurant and discover the secrets behind the region's famous sticky toffee pudding.(n)

programme.title: Ainsley's Mediterranean Cookbook
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott is still in the eastern Mediterranean country of Jordan. In the seaside city of Aqaba he uses local molasses in a fattoush salad, and cooks with the chefs at a swanky golf club, where roasted fish with spicy rice is the order of the day. Ainsley also visits the city of Petra, learning the history of the Bedouin people and exploring the astonishing Treasury building. Back in his kitchen, chicken shawarma with tahini sauce and pitta bread packed with spiced beef mince are the final dishes on the menu.(n)

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal

programme.title: Ninja Warrior UK
programme.desc: Ben Shephard, Rochelle Humes and Chris Kamara host the grand final of the obstacle course challenge, with the final five competitors from across the nation testing their strength, speed and courage. Those who make it to the end of the adrenaline-fuelled mission face one final task - scaling the heights of a tower named Mount Midoriyama - as they battle it out for the title of Ninja Warrior UK.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host the 14th series of nationwide talent search, which sees a wide variety of acts, from comedians to contortionists, singers to spoon players, compete for the chance to win £250,000 and a coveted slot at the Royal Variety Performance. Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and David Walliams are once again on hand to judge this week's applicants, and each of them has the chance to put their favourite contestant straight through to the live semi-finals by awarding them the Golden Buzzer.(n)

programme.title: Alan Carr's Epic Gameshow
programme.desc: The comedian takes a trip down memory lane as he presents a series of classic TV game shows, which have all been updated with a new final round offering the contestants a chance to win big. The next four weeks will see Alan in charge of The Price Is Right, Take Your Pick, Strike it Lucky and Bullseye. But tonight he steps into the shoes of much-missed funnyman Bruce Forsyth for Play Your Cards Right, which sees four celebrity couples taking a chance on the turn of a single card. The hopefuls are daytime stalwarts Ruth Langsford and Eamonn Holmes, comedian Chris Ramsey and his wife Rosie, presenter Ore Oduba and his wife Portia, and singer-actress Martine McCutcheon and her husband Jack McManus.(n)

Ainsley also visits the city of Petra, learning the history of the Bedouin people and exploring the astonishing Treasury building. programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Isobel suggests using Downton as a place of convalescence following a distressing incident at the village hospital. Shell-shocked new valet Lang arrives as William gets his call-up papers, and Carson strives to keep standards high, but suffers a panic attack in front of the family. Lady Rosamund is eager to unearth Lavinia and Carlisle's secret after overhearing them arguing, and romance blossoms for both Edith and Mary. Costume drama, starring Jim Carter, Samantha Bond and Cal Macaninch.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Farmer Alex Preston is found dead at the sacred Crowcall stone circle in Midsomer Mow. Suspicion initially falls on local druids led by high priests Ezra Canning and Leticia Clifford, since Preston's plans to plough Gorse Meadow would have barred their access to the site. Barnaby and Jones are joined by new pathologist Kate Wilding to investigate, and as the case wears on, the inspector becomes convinced greed rather than spiritualism is the motive. Starring Neil Dudgeon, Jason Hughes and Tamzin Malleson.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The detective is faced with a dilemma when a murder investigation leads him to the radar station where his missing son was working on a highly classified operation - just as Hitler launches a major offensive on Britain's RAF airfields. To solve the mystery, Foyle must penetrate the closed ranks of the airforce. Second World War crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Julian Ovenden, Honeysuckle Weeks, Anthony Calf, Roger Allam and Anton Lesser.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective gains a taste of prison life while investigating the murder of an inmate jailed for a property swindle. The victim's two partners-in-crime are also incarcerated in the same low-security cell block. programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George celebrates his 24th wedding anniversary by presenting Mildred with a carriage clock. However, he has purchased it cut-price from a dubious source, and when the Fourmiles return from holiday they find their home burgled, and among the missing items is a carriage clock. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin uses the threat of a roving mouse as an excuse to spend the night in Chrissy's bedroom. Comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The detective is faced with a dilemma when a murder investigation leads him to the radar station where his missing son was working on a highly classified operation - just as Hitler launches a major offensive on Britain's RAF airfields. Second World War crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Julian Ovenden, Honeysuckle Weeks, Anthony Calf, Roger Allam and Anton Lesser.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A spate of petty thefts is traced to a local nursing home, but Blaketon's investigations uncover something far more sinister. Meanwhile, David's first day in his new job goes spectacularly wrong, but Bernie is not surprised. Rural drama, starring Jason Durr and Derek Fowlds.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Roy lays unconscious after losing a couple of fingers in the saw. Emma tries to force Zoe to have a drink with her and Susie. Frank shocks Tina by inviting her to go on holiday.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy is jealous when Tina tells her that she is going to the West Indies with Frank. Nick catches the poachers and threatens them with a shotgun.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The brewery writes to Bet confirming the sell-off and give her first option to buy the Rovers. Raquel fears for her job and turns to Curly for a shoulder to cry on.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Bill and Raquel start looking for new jobs. Rita considers helping Bet to buy the Rovers. Steve suggests to Vicky that they close down the business and start something more fun.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby visits a new age commune, where a man has fallen to his death, or may have been pushed. The dead man turns out to have a connection to the leader's shady past - they set the commune up together as a confidence trick, but the leader came to believe in what he preaches. A further death raises greater suspicion, especially since a vast inheritance might be coming the group's way. Guest starring Stephen Moyer (True Blood).(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A spate of petty thefts is traced to a local nursing home, but Blaketon's investigations uncover something far more sinister. Meanwhile, David's first day in his new job goes spectacularly wrong, but Bernie is not surprised. Rural drama, starring Jason Durr and Derek Fowlds.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: The wounded officers arrive at Downton and Matthew suggests he bring General Sir Herbert Strutt to inspect the new convalescent home - but Branson decides to spoil the visit after failing his army medical. Meanwhile, William's return results in an awkward surprise for Daisy, and Violet and Rosamund discover Lavinia's past misdeeds. Costume drama, starring Dan Stevens, Allen Leech, Thomas Howes, Maggie Smith and Zoe Boyle.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Pauline grows increasingly frustrated with her role at the surgery and starts to interfere with Martin's work, much to the GP's annoyance. The former surgeon talks to Bert, who is feeling down after his son Al's decision to go travelling, before coming face to face with the village's new police officer. Comedy drama series, starring Martin Clunes.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Bert comes to the conclusion that a new challenge will help him move on from Al's departure and decides to open a restaurant, but a spate of food-poisoning cases coincides with his first day of business. Martin meets Poppy, the surgery's new receptionist, and Pauline returns from her course. Comedy drama, starring Ian McNeice and Martin Clunes.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The year is 1940, and Sam is made homeless when a bomb destroys her boarding house, alerting Foyle to a system of organised looting around Hastings. However, when a corpse is found on the beach, the investigation brings the team into conflict with British attempts to secure vital American aid for the war effort. Police drama, starring Michael Kitchen, with Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The detective discovers his half-sister's daughter has committed suicide and sets out to find what prompted it, entering the world of teenage rave parties and drug-taking. Meanwhile, Lewis has his sights set on promotion and studies for exams. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Sorcha Cusack, Jason Isaacs and John Junkin.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The girls find it tough sharing with one man, but their problems double once Robin's friend Larry moves in. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team head to Chessington to create a car-themed garden for hospital worker and automobile fan Alan Rendall. In July 2014, he managed to rescue his unconscious neighbour, Roy Whiteway, from his burning house, but was unable to save Dorothy, Roy's wife, as the fire was so intense. To recognise his bravery, Alan and the team surprise the whole Rendall family and turn their featureless plot into an automobile-inspired haven.(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team are in Hemel Hempstead, Hertfordshire, where they meet deaf couple Asif Iqbal and wife Sabina. The green-fingered experts attempt to turn a boring lawn and crumbling patio into a Bollywood dream garden with an outdoor kitchen for Asif, Sabina and their family.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Oliver's recovery hits a setback and David continues his quest for kicks when he approaches some youths on bikes and purposely shoves one of them over. James struggles with locker-room banter and Gemma shows Chesney her vlog, but makes it clear she has no intention of putting it online after her humiliating turnstile experience.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: David is chased by an angry mob and cornered in an alleyway, while the doctor warns Leanne that it is too soon to be sure, but Oliver may have suffered brain damage. Tommy Orpington assures James he did the right thing coming out, and Chesney urges Gemma to share her vlog online.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Shona visits Gail and Sarah, but is clearly confused, while Alya visits Yasmeen in prison and assures her that she knows Geoff abused her, but Yasmeen refuses to discuss it. The doctor tells Leanne that Oliver's test results won't be back for a few weeks and he's free to go home, but while Leanne's delighted, convinced he's improving, Steve and Nick mask their concern.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: English country squire Marcus Waverly receives a series of anonymous letters threatening to kidnap his son Johnnie, but the case is dismissed by Scotland Yard - prompting Poirot to open his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet and Geoffrey Bateman.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Ghosts from the past return to haunt Morse as he investigates the apparent suicide of a terminally ill Oxford don whose widow turns out to be the woman the detective was set to marry in his youth. As the case develops, Lewis worries his partner's professionalism is being undermined. Starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Joanna David, Samantha Bond, David Haig, Adrian Dunbar and James Grout.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Up the Khyber (1968)
programme.desc: British forces in colonial India find themselves in deep trouble when a manic local ruler discovers the soldiers' fearsome reputation is thoroughly undeserved - prompting open rebellion. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey, Roy Castle, Joan Sims, Bernard Bresslaw and Terry Scott.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The murder of a well-known business tycoon leads Morse to investigate the man's feuding family, and inadvertently makes him the unwitting target of a hate campaign by the tabloids - a problem compounded by his uneasy relationship with a new chief superintendent. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star alongside Gwen Taylor, Martin Clunes, Alun Armstrong, Anna Massey, Charlotte Coleman and Rupert Graves.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A walk in the woods leads to tragedy for Emily Simpson, who witnesses something unsettling before being found dead in her cottage by old friend Lucy Bellringer. Convinced natural causes are not to blame, Lucy calls in DCI Barnaby and Sergeant Troy, whose investigation eventually uncovers sinister long-buried secrets. The first ever episode of the long-running detective drama, guest starring Jessica Hynes and Emily Mortimer.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Farmer Alex Preston is found dead at the sacred Crowcall stone circle in Midsomer Mow. Suspicion initially falls on local druids led by high priests Ezra Canning and Leticia Clifford, since Preston's plans to plough Gorse Meadow would have barred their access to the site. Barnaby and Jones are joined by new pathologist Kate Wilding to investigate, and as the case wears on, the inspector becomes convinced greed rather than spiritualism is the motive. Starring Neil Dudgeon, Jason Hughes and Tamzin Malleson.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A local woman is found murdered following a row with her husband at a residents' association Spanish-themed evening. The finger of suspicion soon points to an odd-job man with a violent history, and the chairman of the association reveals he heard a squeaking noise after the victim left - but the witness promptly winds up dead himself. Crime drama, starring John Nettles.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Leaving behind his beloved Oxford, Morse and a reluctant Lewis set off to investigate the bizarre death of an Englishwoman who was attending a psychotherapy clinic in northern Italy. Their inquiries lead them to the vineyards and villas of Vicenza and the city of Verona - as well as a glamorous opera singer and a convicted fraudster. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star as the detective duo, with Frances Barber, Michael Kitchen, James Grout and Jolyon Baker.(n)

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Billy apologises to Betty for embarrassing her. Curly realises something is wrong with Raquel and she reluctantly tells him what happened with Leo in the store room.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Raquel reacts violently when Bill goes to give her a hug. Curly has to stop himself from attacking Leo. Betty tells Gordon that she is getting married.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Susie is still annoying Susie by her presence. Jack forgets Sarah's birthday. Nick is attacked when he catches the poachers red-handed. Kathy asks Sean to stay the night.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Dingles think Zak has convinced Nellie to come home. Kim's solicitor has some bad news for her. Emma and Zoe's night out is interrupted by Susie.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Aidensfield residents are shocked when an MI5 agent turns up in the village showing more than just a passing interest in the activities of Vernon Scripps and his mysterious associate, a man who disembarked from a Russian trawler docked in Whitby harbour. It seems there is an even darker side to the roguish wheeler-dealer than they suspected.(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The Ropers' television breaks down, and fixing it is a question of whether the British Museum has the parts in stock. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce.(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Poor George feels outnumbered when he learns Mildred's mother could be moving in. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.sub-title: I Won't Dance, Don't Ask Me
programme.desc: Chrissy needs a partner for the annual staff dance, so Robin takes lessons from Mrs Roper. Flat-sharing comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan, Paula Wilcox and Brian Murphy.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The unassuming detective launches an investigation when a conscientious objector dies while in police custody, and the judge who ordered his arrest is nearly killed in his booby-trapped home. His inquiries uncover a web of lies and corruption in the heart of wartime Hastings. Crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and David Tennant.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Community spirit in the village of Morton Fendle is dealt a blow by the collapse of a craft centre in which many locals had invested - and Alan Hollingsworth, the businessman behind the project, has become a public hate figure. Barnaby investigates when Hollingsworth's wife disappears, and seems to have been kidnapped. However, when Hollingsworth is subsequently murdered and his wife reappears safe and sound, he suspects an elaborate scheme. Roger Allam guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Isobel suggests using Downton as a place of convalescence following a distressing incident at the village hospital. Lady Rosamund is eager to unearth Lavinia and Carlisle's secret after overhearing them arguing, and romance blossoms for both Edith and Mary. Costume drama, starring Jim Carter, Samantha Bond and Cal Macaninch.(n)

programme.desc: A walk in the woods leads to tragedy for Emily Simpson, who witnesses something unsettling before being found dead in her cottage by old friend Lucy Bellringer. Convinced natural causes are not to blame, Lucy calls in DCI Barnaby and Sergeant Troy, whose investigation eventually uncovers sinister long-buried secrets. The first ever episode of the long-running detective drama, guest starring Jessica Hynes and Emily Mortimer.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Farmer Alex Preston is found dead at the sacred Crowcall stone circle in Midsomer Mow. Suspicion initially falls on local druids led by high priests Ezra Canning and Leticia Clifford, since Preston's plans to plough Gorse Meadow would have barred their access to the site. Barnaby and Jones are joined by new pathologist Kate Wilding to investigate, and as the case wears on, the inspector becomes convinced greed rather than spiritualism is the motive. Starring Neil Dudgeon, Jason Hughes and Tamzin Malleson.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A local woman is found murdered following a row with her husband at a residents' association Spanish-themed evening. The finger of suspicion soon points to an odd-job man with a violent history, and the chairman of the association reveals he heard a squeaking noise after the victim left - but the witness promptly winds up dead himself. Crime drama, starring John Nettles.(n)

programme.title: World of Sport: TV Firsts
programme.desc: Richard Henwood looks back at television sporting firsts, including footage of a 14-year-old Ronnie O'Sullivan who would go on to take snooker by storm.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A teenager dies after taking a seemingly harmless pill, prompting ever-vigilant Quincy to launch a campaign to stop its production and close down a local shop that sells the pills.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A teenager accidentally kills one of a mobster's henchmen during a robbery gone wrong, and ends up being hired by a rival gangster who wants the young crook to eliminate his rivals.(n)

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Suffering from nervous exhaustion, Holmes sets off for a break on the wild Cornish peninsula, where he hopes the peace and quiet will help him convalesce. Unfortunately, several murders ensure his holiday is far from relaxing. Period detective drama, guest starring Denis Quilley.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur comes up with a plan to boost business at the Winchester Club by staging variety nights. The idea proves popular until the karaoke machine, rented from the fearsome Gabadini family, is stolen. George Cole stars.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle and Bodie pose as hitmen to infiltrate a mysterious group of hire killers whose targets are all retired government officials, and supposedly no longer of any strategic importance. Adventure, with Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The pathologist goes on a campaign to clean up the Los Angeles air after an elderly man and a young boy die during an atmospheric inversion.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A gang of thieves go after their hidden loot from a robbery a year previously - but the only member of the gang who knows where it is dies suddenly. Armand Assante guest stars.(n)

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A houseguest wakes up in dilapidated Wisteria Lodge to find that his host and all the household servants have vanished. Understandably perplexed, he heads straight for Baker Street and asks Holmes to solve the mystery of their disappearance. Jeremy Brett and Edward Hardwicke star.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Boxer Lewis Nelson hires Ray to keep his gambling-addicted wife away from the casino he partly owns. However, she persuades Arthur to play the roulette wheels on her behalf, tempting him with the 'winning system' she has devised. Comedy starring George Cole.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A former agent regresses to a time when he was captured and brain-washed by the KGB into killing four of his colleagues. After escaping from a mental hospital, he sets out to resume his mission, leaving Bodie and Doyle with the task of trying to protect them. Action drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins.(n)

programme.title: When English Football Ruled Europe
programme.desc: A look at English clubs' dominance of the European Cup between 1977 and 1984. The second programme looks at the breakup of Brian Clough's hugely successful Nottingham Forest, and the re-emergence of Liverpool, who lifted their third and fourth European cups in 1981 and 1984. Plus, the incredible story of Aston Villa, who upset all the odds to claim their one and only win in the competition in 1982.(n)

programme.title: Euro 96 Relived
programme.sub-title: Czech Republic v Germany: Euro 96 Relived
programme.desc: A chance to see the final, at Wembley Stadium, as Germany looked to go one better than four years ago against the tournament's surprise package. The sides also met in their opening group game, with the Germans prevailing 2-0, but for England fans it was a case of looking on in envy and wondering what might have been, following their agonising defeat to Germany on penalties in the semi-finals. Bob Wilson hosted the coverage, with analysis from Terry Venables, Alex Ferguson and Kevin Keegan.(n)

programme.title: When Football Changed Forever
programme.desc: The dramatic story of the 1991/92 Football League Division One season - the final campaign before the Premier League began. Leeds United overcame fierce rivals Manchester United to emerge as champions, thanks to a certain Frenchman named Eric Cantona.(n)

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.sub-title: The Man with the Twisted Lip
programme.desc: A woman asks Holmes to search for her missing husband, a businessman who was last seen peering from the window of an opium den in London's notorious Limehouse area. Suspicion falls on a well-known local beggar - but his innocence is eventually proved in bizarre fashion. Period drama, starring Jeremy Brett.(n)

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes: The Hound of the Baskervilles (2000)
programme.desc: The sleuth and his faithful assistant Dr Watson accompany the last remaining heir to the Baskerville fortune to his ancestral Dartmoor home, where they protect him from a monstrous spectral hound that is rumoured to prowl the wild Devon heath and is hunting down the family. Mystery based on Arthur Conan Doyle's story, starring Matt Frewer, Jason London, Kenneth Welsh and Emma Campbell.(n)

programme.title: The Cowboys (1972)
programme.desc: An ageing rancher finds himself short-handed when his workers abandon him during a gold rush, and grudgingly hires a group of schoolboys to help him on a 400-mile cattle drive. Although at first they feel out of their depth, the youngsters quickly learn to work like men - but face a dangerous challenge ahead as a gang of rustlers begins to stalk them. Western, with John Wayne, Roscoe Lee Browne and Bruce Dern.(n)

programme.title: Guns of the Magnificent Seven (1969)
programme.desc: Surviving gunslinger Chris is hired by Mexican revolutionaries to rescue their leader from a military prison. After assembling a crack team including a knife-fighting expert and a firearms aficionado, the group embarks on its mission - but underestimates the depths to which its enemies will sink. Second sequel to the Western, starring George Kennedy, James Whitmore, Monte Markham, Joe Don Baker and Reni Santoni.(n)

programme.desc: An ageing rancher finds himself short-handed when his workers abandon him during a gold rush, and grudgingly hires a group of schoolboys to help him on a 400-mile cattle drive. Although at first they feel out of their depth, the youngsters quickly learn to work like men - but face a dangerous challenge ahead as a gang of rustlers begins to stalk them. Western, with John Wayne, Roscoe Lee Browne and Bruce Dern.(n)

programme.title: Guns of the Magnificent Seven (1969)
programme.desc: Surviving gunslinger Chris is hired by Mexican revolutionaries to rescue their leader from a military prison. After assembling a crack team including a knife-fighting expert and a firearms aficionado, the group embarks on its mission - but underestimates the depths to which its enemies will sink. Second sequel to the Western, starring George Kennedy, James Whitmore, Monte Markham, Joe Don Baker and Reni Santoni.(n)

First World War drama, starring Malcolm McDowell, Christopher Plummer, Simon Ward and Peter Firth.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Best Parent?
programme.desc: Twelve families with differing parenting styles compete against each other, hoping to prove that their methods and techniques are the right way to raise children. Those taking part in the first episode include Joana, who believes children shouldn't be confined to traditional gender stereotypes and is raising her five-year-old with a gender-fluid approach. Self-titled lazy parents Kevin and Kerry believe that their hands-off style makes their two boys self-sufficient and aware of the consequences of their actions, while Rin and Robin's parenting style is inspired by Eastern culture and philosophy, as they aspire to make 'scholar-warriors' of their son and daughter. Presented by Anita Rani.(n)

Others looking for a little fun in the sun are chef James and actor Jamie, and care home manager Hayley, who describes her ideal man as a 'Viking' and confident. programme.title: The Imposter (2012)
programme.desc: Bafta-winning documentary about the case of Nicholas Barclay, a 13-year-old boy who went missing, and the man who claimed to be the youngster years later, having apparently escaped being held captive. His radically different appearance and personality raised suspicion, but the child's family accepted him unquestioningly. Featuring contributions from the people involved in the case and filmed re-enactments of key events.(n)

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In

The pair go on a guided tour in which they view masterpieces by Bosch, Velasquez, Picasso and Dali, before they attend a ham-carving workshop and a sample a five-course tasting menu, of which four courses are pork or ham, including a tartare dish. They also take in a lively boating lake, a circus church and a grisly tour of the Spanish Inquisition.(n)

programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of musician Toby Leeming as he sets out to build a house wrapped around an ancient oak tree in rural Suffolk. The design for Toby's five-building home contains six bedrooms, six bathrooms, a music studio and an annexe - a vast project more like a village than a house. Creating this life changing vision on a £530k budget, and in only eight months, will not be an easy task, especially as they must build up close to an ancient oak without destroying any of the roots.(n)

programme.title: D-Day: The King Who Fooled Hitler
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the role played by George VI in maintaining the secrecy of the plans for the D-Day Landings, and how the royal family regained the trust of British intelligence, following concerns about Edward VIII's reputation as a Nazi sympathiser. The programme also examines how the Queen Mother preserved one of the greatest secrets of the Second World War.(n)

programme.title: Guy Martin's D-Day Landing
programme.desc: The presenter undertakes a challenge to reenact the kind of parachute jumps undertaken by 23,000 Allied soldiers during D-Day. The first step is to restore one of the planes used in these missions, before undergoing training with the Red Devils parachute display team and the modern parachute regiment. Guy is then ready to make his a descent into the Normandy countryside. Featuring contributions from veterans including a flying nurse, a special forces pathfinder, and a German soldier.(n)

programme.desc: Documentary following the endearing characters who banded together to keep Bressingham Steam Museum in Norfolk - which is run as a charity - going. Brian the museum's carpenter repairs a golden cockerel for the Victorian gallopers, but struggles when it comes to fitting it.(n)

programme.title: Fare Dodgers: At War with the Law
programme.desc: A prolific offender is landed with an eyewatering bill to square off three years of illegal travel, while the police must step in when one suspect is found in possession of a banned substance. At one of London's busiest stations, vigilant inspectors Scott and Michelle have success intercepting repeat offenders, but will they catch a notorious passenger who the station staff report comes through their station each day?(n)

programme.title: Paddington Station 24/7
programme.sub-title: Paddington: A Year on the Tracks
programme.desc: Autumn gets off to a wet start when the roof of Paddington begins to leak during torrential rainfall, and with gales of up to 90mph, Network Rail staff brace themselves as a storm closes in on the UK. A flyaway plastic bag causes a loss of power, trapping three trains and disrupting Paddington's peak service. Mobile operations manager Jane is called out when a van hits a low-level bridge, while British Transport Police in Bristol Temple Meads get more than they bargained for when they search a teenager suspected of using drugs on board a train.(n)

programme.title: The Tube: Going Underground
programme.desc: At Leicester Square station, customer service assistants Ric and Kingsley are faced with crowds of ghosts, ghouls and demons as Halloween partygoers pass through, while the emergency response team is sent to St John's Wood after passengers report hearing gunfire. Meanwhile at King's Cross, engineer Pete leads a massive weekend track-replacement operation, but he and his team hit an unexpected problem.(n)

programme.title: Police: Hour of Duty
programme.desc: Officers search for a man reported missing by his partner, while detectives question a man who is alleged to have been grooming a 14-year-old boy. Back at Comms HQ, the force incident manager coordinates a response to assist a teenager who has been stabbed, and armed officers are sent to search and arrest the suspects at their premises, where they also discover a cannabis factory.(n)
